summary: The concept of using breasts for pediatric atopy management lacks any established clinical rationale; instead, research indicates that breastfeeding may offer immune benefits unrelated directly to reducing atopic disease risk due to familial predisposition. Atopy’s hereditary nature does not support recommending specific body parts such as breasts for intervention.
